name: WEAVE Spec Audit And Gated Delivery Checklist
gating_rule: Each story gate must be true before its dependent story is treated as executable.

user_stories.title: Run a local-first demo workspace surface
user_stories.user_story: As a new evaluator, I can start WEAVE locally and open a believable demo workspace with theme tokens and live data.
implemented: Axum relay server boots against the demo workspace fixture.
Svelte frontend loads dashboard, pages, feed, directories, libraries, courses, agents, exports, notifications, and previews.
Theme tokens and demo fixture content are wired into the primary surface.
missing: Desktop packaging is still outside this story.
Workspace switching is not handled here.

acceptance_criteria.description: The relay can boot using the demo workspace root.
The frontend renders the demo workspace from live API responses.
The demo workspace acts as an acceptance fixture rather than dead sample data.

user_stories.title: Load canonical files and support revisioned editorial writes
user_stories.user_story: As an editor, I can load canonical workspace files, save drafts, publish immutable page revisions, and append feed posts into JSONL segments.
implemented: Workspace repository reads pages, drafts, revisions, directories, libraries, courses, feed segments, agents, automations, exports, and notifications from canonical files.
Page publishing writes immutable revision files and updates the published pointer.
Draft saving writes user-scoped draft files.
Feed posting appends canonical JSONL events.
missing: Canonical envelope fields are still thin relative to the full file contract.
Comments, likes, mentions, and moderation are not part of this story yet.

acceptance_criteria.description: Canonical page records are read from ordinary files inside the workspace root.
Draft and publish flows write canonical files rather than local-only state.
High-churn social activity uses append-only JSONL segments.

user_stories.title: Audit canonical state and rebuild disposable local cache
user_stories.user_story: As an operator, I can audit workspace integrity, see sync health, and rebuild a disposable local cache outside the canonical Drive workspace.
implemented: Workspace audit now checks required paths, missing references, orphaned drafts, conflict copies, and Lost and Found items.
Sync health now surfaces stale cache families, audit issue counts, Lost and Found counts, export backlog, decryption state, relay state, and last cache rebuild time.
Cache rebuild now writes a disposable SQLite cache outside the canonical workspace root.
Operator CLI entrypoints and scripts now expose audit and rebuild output.
The frontend now exposes workspace-engine health, audit summaries, and cache rebuild actions.
missing: Drive change-log replay and live watchers are still future work.
Conflict merge flows and repair UI remain to be built.

acceptance_criteria.description: A machine-readable audit report can be produced from the workspace root.
A rebuildable cache is written outside the canonical workspace tree.
Sync health is visible through API and UI rather than implied.

user_stories.title: Ship a thin desktop shell and operator setup flow
user_stories.user_story: As a new user, I can open the desktop shell, select a workspace root, verify `gws`, configure Gemini, and begin from a supported local setup flow.
implemented: Bootstrap diagnostics now surface `gws` presence, Gemini env readiness, the active workspace root, and the demo root.
The selected workspace root can now be switched from the product surface and persisted outside the canonical workspace.
A thin Tauri desktop shell scaffold now exists and compiles against the shared Rust runtime.
Doctor and local dev scripts exist.
missing: Native file-picker UX for workspace-root selection.
`gws` auth wizard and version pin UX.
Gemini setup flow beyond environment inspection.

acceptance_criteria.description: The product ships a runnable Tauri desktop shell.
Users can choose and persist a Drive-backed workspace root.
Bootstrap UX verifies `gws` and Gemini setup in-product.

blocking_items: Add a native file-picker flow for workspace selection.
Add bootstrap flows for `gws` auth and Gemini configuration.

user_stories.title: Deliver the structured page and guide system
user_stories.user_story: As an editor, I can compose rich structured pages, templates, and guides with reusable block behavior rather than a thin heading-plus-callout editor.
implemented: Page records are block-based JSON documents.
The current editor can write a basic heading block and callout block.
missing: Renderer and editor support for the required core block catalog.
Guide trees and persistent guide outline behavior.
Template library and clone-to-template flow.
Google-native blocks and export-specific block behavior.
Revision comparison, translation, archive, and Doc import reconciliation.

acceptance_criteria.description: Required core blocks are implemented with renderer and editor behavior.
Guides are first-class page trees with persistent outline behavior.
Templates cover the required editorial starting points.

blocking_items: The block system is still a narrow subset of the spec.

user_stories.title: Build the full home and navigation experience
user_stories.user_story: As a member, I can open a complete home surface with the required modules and strong navigation primitives.
implemented: The dashboard includes feed, search, directories, libraries, learning, agents, notifications, exports, and previews.
missing: Trending hashtags, quick links, birthdays, anniversaries, upcoming events, recently visited, best bets, breadcrumbs, mega menu, community switcher, right-rail related content, and Google affordances.

acceptance_criteria.description: Home includes the required editorial and social modules.
Navigation includes mega menu, breadcrumbs, community switching, and content outliner behavior.

user_stories.title: Ship full document, video, and image libraries
user_stories.user_story: As a knowledge manager, I can browse rich Drive-backed libraries with views, metadata, previews, and import-export tooling.
implemented: Document and video library metadata load from canonical files.
Library items can appear in UI cards and search results.
missing: Image library support.
Saved views, sort, filter, grouping, thumbnails, batch import, sheet workflows, and linked Google file indexing.

acceptance_criteria.description: Documents, videos, and images all have first-class library support.
Views, filters, previews, and import-export flows are available.

blocking_items: Images and operational library tooling are still absent.

user_stories.title: Deliver rich directories, profiles, and connector ingestion
user_stories.user_story: As an operator, I can manage employees, projects, and imported records through rich directory and profile surfaces.
implemented: Employee and project records load from canonical files and render in the UI.
Directory records can be searched.
missing: Companies, contacts, opportunities, and course directories.
Saved views, map mode, hover cards, custom fields, relationship editing, profile hydration, and connector ingestion.

acceptance_criteria.description: The required directory families exist with rich browse modes.
Profiles can hydrate from Google sources and extend locally.
Optional connectors normalize imported records into canonical structures.

blocking_items: Directory scope is still limited to employees and projects.

user_stories.title: Complete the social feed, notifications, and digest system
user_stories.user_story: As a member, I can post, discuss, react, follow hashtags, and receive notifications and digests across the supported channels.
implemented: Feed posts can be created and promoted.
Notifications have canonical fixture records.
Automation recipes and Google previews hint at digest and chat mirror flows.
missing: Comments, likes, mentions, hashtag pages, moderation, scheduling, per-user preferences, digest generation rules, Gmail delivery, and Chat mirror execution.

acceptance_criteria.description: Discussion, reaction, and hashtag flows are fully canonical and visible.
Digest logic is reproducible and auditable.
Gmail and Chat delivery paths are available when enabled.

blocking_items: The feed model only covers post creation today.

user_stories.title: Ship full local-first retrieval and answer UX
user_stories.user_story: As a member, I can search everything locally and get fast cited answers with tabs, filters, and exhaustive mode.
implemented: Local search spans pages, posts, library items, directory entities, and courses.
The answer pane returns visible citations.
The UI already exposes a grounded answer panel.
missing: BM25, vectors, facet tabs, exhaustive mode, permission-aware filtering, richer citations, linked Google file indexing, and result quality blending.

acceptance_criteria.description: Retrieval blends lexical and semantic ranking with permissions and freshness.
The UI exposes result tabs, filters, and exhaustive mode.
Citations deep-link to anchored source locations.

blocking_items: Search is still a grounded but lightweight substring implementation.

user_stories.title: Build the real Gemini broker
user_stories.user_story: As an operator, I can route reasoning through a centralized Gemini broker with aliases, tools, history, and grounded answer synthesis.
implemented: A small broker crate exists and centralizes current answer synthesis.
missing: Model aliases, tool declarations, history, caching, usage accounting, streaming, retries, and thought-signature preservation.

acceptance_criteria.description: Gemini model aliasing is centralized and used across the repo.
Tool orchestration, caching, and grounded synthesis are first-class runtime features.

blocking_items: The broker is still a placeholder rather than the full runtime.

user_stories.title: Make agents active first-class workspace members
user_stories.user_story: As a member, I can see useful agent activity with visible traces, scoped tools, and canonical outputs.
implemented: Agent definitions and prompts are canonical files.
Agent cards render in the UI.
missing: Agent runtime, thread state, memory policy, visible action traces, tool enforcement, and canonical agent output flows.

acceptance_criteria.description: Agents can execute the required actions with visible traces.
Permission scope is enforced at tool boundaries.

blocking_items: Agents exist as records, not as a live system.

user_stories.title: Ship the learning, video, and live-learning stack
user_stories.user_story: As a learner, I can browse courses and videos, see assignments, search transcripts, and join live sessions tied to Calendar and Meet.
implemented: Course records and live session records load from canonical files.
The UI exposes courses and live learning cards.
missing: Video player, captions, transcripts, chapters, analytics, assignments, learning paths, transcript entries, and deeper Calendar and Meet flows.

acceptance_criteria.description: Required learning objects and video features are available in-product.
Learning reminders, rosters, and profile summaries are integrated.

blocking_items: Learning data exists, but the actual learning experience is still thin.

user_stories.title: Complete Docs, Sheets, Slides, Gmail, Calendar, and Chat coordination
user_stories.user_story: As an operator, I can export, import, deliver, mirror, and schedule Google-side work with visible command shadows and canonical backlinks.
implemented: Recipe previews and Google action previews exist for Docs, Gmail, Calendar, Chat, Sheets, and Slides shadows.
Export records are canonical fixture data.
missing: Real Docs export/import flows, Sheets bulk-edit workflows, Slides generation, Gmail send/draft execution, Calendar writes, Chat mirroring, and Groups/Admin sync.

acceptance_criteria.description: Google-side workflows execute with canonical linkage and dry-run shadows.
Docs, Sheets, and Slides import-export flows are live rather than preview-only.

blocking_items: The repo stops at dry-run previews today.

user_stories.title: Deliver full branding controls and accessibility support
user_stories.user_story: As a workspace admin, I can tune brand feel while keeping the product accessible across light, dark, motion, and keyboard usage.
implemented: The current frontend has a custom visual language, theme tokens, and mobile-aware layout behavior.
missing: Dark mode, reduced motion, explicit branding controls, contrast automation, extension surfaces, and theme export consistency.

acceptance_criteria.description: Brand controls cover the required theme and navigation settings.
Accessibility requirements are implemented and tested.

blocking_items: Branding and accessibility are still mostly static styling.

user_stories.title: Implement privacy, encryption, analytics, and governance surfaces
user_stories.user_story: As an admin, I can keep private scopes private, inspect analytics and audit trails, and export governance-friendly data.
missing: Encryption model, key wrapping, key rotation, permission-aware indexing, analytics API, JSON/tabular exports, Drive label mirroring, and governance surfaces.

acceptance_criteria.description: Private scopes are encrypted and excluded from unauthorized indexing.
Analytics, audit, and governance APIs exist with local-first posture.

blocking_items: This area is still absent from the implementation.

user_stories.title: Evolve the relay from local API to optional background runtime
user_stories.user_story: As an operator, I can run the optional relay for browser mode, webhooks, schedules, background jobs, and operations visibility without making it the source of truth.
implemented: A local relay binary exists as the API surface.
missing: Browser UI, webhook receiver, scheduled recipe execution, background delivery jobs, background transcription, and operations dashboard.

acceptance_criteria.description: Relay responsibilities beyond the local API are implemented and optional.

blocking_items: The relay is still a local API service rather than the optional extended runtime.

user_stories.title: Finish repository scaffolding, CI, release, and visual quality gates
user_stories.user_story: As a maintainer, I can rely on complete repo scaffolding, repeatable checks, screenshots, CI, and release packaging.
implemented: Root and nested AGENTS files exist in the required critical subtrees.
Doctor, dev, lint, test, security, audit, and rebuild scripts exist.
Rust and frontend wrapper commands are runnable.
missing: CI configuration, screenshot fixtures, e2e suite, markdown/docs linting, release packaging scripts, and golden comparison workflow.

acceptance_criteria.description: Repository scaffolding fully matches the target layout and required scripts.
CI, visual parity, release packaging, and security automation are complete.

blocking_items: Release and CI surfaces are not finished yet.
